published_prior_art.cite: ArnoldBerrieTennantGilthorpe2020 + TomovaGilthorpeTennant2022 define compositional relative/substitution effects; VanderWeeleHernan2013 identifies version-specific effects, but none verified gives the exact support-span collapse certificate
HiranoImbens2004 identifies continuous-treatment dose-response surfaces under weak unconfoundedness; Piepel2006/Piepel2007 already make direction-dependent mixture slopes explicit
HiranoImbens2004 + HinesDiazOrdazVansteelandt2026: ordinary continuous-treatment derivative recovery under local overlap is background
Two-component simplex has only one displaced component; this is a sanity reduction, not a novelty claim

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; Piepel2007 Component Slope Linear Model is a closer missed mixture-experiment comparator for direction-indexed component slopes, and the current kernel remains an elementary support-span restatement unless upgraded with a nonroutine causal identification or inference contribution.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The theorem says endpoints solve linear programs over a support-admissible reallocation polytope, but Assumption 5 only makes Q_j(a) nonempty and compact; convexity/polyhedrality is not assumed.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: For a flagship-aspirant kernel, the support-span iff reduces to unfolding tau_j and L_j plus finite-dimensional orthogonal projection; the §10 chain has fewer than three nonroutine steps beyond definitions and standard adjustment.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add an explicit polyhedral local-support assumption, or weaken Theorem 1 from LP endpoints to extrema of an affine map over compact Q_j(a).
To reach flagship, add a nonroutine estimator/inference layer: an observable support-span test or efficient estimator for kappa_j(a) with a rate/limit law under local simplex overlap.
Add Piepel2007 as a named comparator and state exactly why the causal support-span certificate is more than the known component-slope direction choice in mixture experiments.

recommended_next_step: Patch the Q_j(a) polytope assumption and either upgrade the kernel with a genuine inference/identification frontier or downgrade the target from flagship to field.
